title: Cotula pygmaea
intro: Cotula pygmaea, commonly known as Dwarf Cotula or Australian Bushy Daisy, is a small perennial herb native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including skin problems, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cotula pygmaea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cotula pygmaea is often used in preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cotula pygmaea is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within Asteraceae, Cotula pygmaea is a member of the subfamily Asteroideae. The subfamily Asteroideae is further divided into several tribes, with Cotula pygmaea belonging to the tribe Gnaphalieae. The species Cotula pygmaea is also known as the dwarf cotula or pigmy cotula.
uses: Cotula pygmaea is known for its potential use in traditional medicine, particularly in South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves and roots are used in herbal remedies. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. Further studies are essential to understand the full scope of Cotula pygmaea's medicinal properties.
benefits: Cotula pygmaea is often used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which could aid in reducing oxidative stress and protecting against cell damage. Additionally, Cotula pygmaea may have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections and promote a healthy balance of gut bacteria. More research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Cotula pygmaea.

side_effects: Cotula pygmaea, also known as buttonweed, can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals upon contact with its sap.
